expected_behavior: Introduces what the skill does: classify AI work before choosing a model
Explains R.E.S.E.T. (Right-size, Evaluate, Spend, Enforce, Test) in one short paragraph
Shows one example command
Does not produce a recommendation card or JSON because no use case was given
Explains what the skill does, when to use it, and what input to provide
Shows short example usages
Does not produce a recommendation or JSON
Explains the five R.E.S.E.T. lenses: Right-size, Evaluate, Spend, Enforce, and Test
Stays vendor-neutral and does not name models or providers
Does not classify a specific use case
Describes the JSON fields and what each field means
Notes the canonical field is recommended_ai_route with its allowed route values
Does not create or save a file
Shows several sample use cases and the likely route type each would receive
Uses generic route types (for example low_cost_tier, mid_tier, approved_enterprise_route) rather than named models

query: We want to extract invoice totals from scanned invoices. Data includes vendor names, amounts, and invoice numbers. High volume, batch processing is fine.
expected_behavior: Returns a readable recommendation card first, then inline JSON conforming to the schema
Recommends a low-cost or approved route appropriate to the financial data sensitivity
Includes schema validation or sampled QA and cost controls suited to high-volume batch work
Does not name vendors, products, or specific models

query: We want to summarize customer support calls and draft CRM follow-up notes. Data includes customer conversations and possible PII. High volume, latency is not critical.
expected_behavior: Returns a recommendation card plus inline JSON
Classifies data_sensitivity as customer and recommends an approved enterprise (or approved mid-tier) route, putting governance before cost
Includes PII handling and audit logging guardrails, a required eval, and batching cost controls
Does not name vendors, products, or specific models

query: /ai-route-advisor --json-only We want an AI agent that can review pull requests, run tests, and open changes against internal repositories.
expected_behavior: Returns only the JSON object conforming to the schema, with no recommendation card
Sets complexity to high and data_sensitivity to confidential for source code
Recommends an approved or human-review route with tool restrictions and human approval
Includes red-team or required evaluation in the evaluation_plan

query: /ai-route-advisor --card-only Draft internal marketing copy from approved product briefs. Low sensitivity, medium volume.
expected_behavior: Returns only the human-readable recommendation card, with no JSON block
Recommends a low-cost or mid-tier route given low sensitivity and routine drafting
Keeps guardrails proportionate to low-risk internal work

query: /ai-route-advisor Evaluate whether we should replace our current summarization route for internal meeting notes. Data is internal and volume is medium.
expected_behavior: Recommends testing the proposed route in an evaluation_queue before replacing the current workflow
States the new route should be compared against the current route using the same evaluation criteria
Returns a card plus inline JSON

query: /ai-route-advisor We want an AI assistant to review vendor contracts and flag risky clauses. Legal will review the final output.
expected_behavior: Recommends a human_review_route because legal review and high impact are involved
Includes a human approval guardrail before the output is used
Recommends an evaluation plan covering legal accuracy and risk-flag quality

query: /ai-route-advisor --validate-json {"use_case":"extract invoice totals","task_type":"extraction","complexity":"low","data_sensitivity":"confidential","volume":"high","latency_need":"batch","business_impact":"medium","recommended_ai_route":"low_cost_tier","why_this_route":"simple structured extraction","required_guardrails":["schema_validation"],"evaluation_plan":["field_accuracy"],"cost_controls":["batching"],"escalation_rule":"escalate on low confidence","decision":"pilot low-cost route"}
expected_behavior: Validates the pasted JSON against the bundled schema
Only runs scripts/validate_recommendation.py if local execution is available and the user asked for validation
Reports whether the JSON is valid and flags any schema problems

query: /ai-route-advisor --estimate-cost Estimate cost for 2000 input tokens and 500 output tokens across 1000 requests at $1 per million input and $5 per million output.
expected_behavior: Estimates a rough cost because token counts, request volume, and pricing assumptions were provided
Uses scripts/estimate_cost.py when local execution is available
Frames cost as cost per correct outcome and notes retries or review effort where relevant
